title: Inplace fused (leaky)relu+(leaky)dropout for memory savings

root_cause.mechanism: Feature request — Inductor does fuse relu+dropout into a single kernel, but does not produce fully inplace/allocation-less code. The backward pass requires saving either the input or the relu mask, preventing full elimination of intermediate buffers. The high watermark of memory is determined by saved-for-backward tensors, not intermediate allocations.

diagnostic_path.conclusion: Fundamental autograd constraint prevents full inplace

diagnostic_path.result: Chillee explained this would require saving output + reading it back, more expensive than mask

diagnostic_path.action: Benchmarked manual inplace impl vs torch.compile
diagnostic_path.result: torch.compile gives equal or better performance
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Inductor fusion already near-optimal for speed

diagnostic_path.result: Inplace intermediates don't help with peak memory, which is dominated by activations saved for backward

title: [AOTI] Severe Performance Regression with FP16 Autocast in AOTInductor for Small Batch Sizes

root_cause.mechanism: Linear decomposition's bmm-vs-mm folding heuristic fails for fp16 with small batch sizes. When batch_first=True, transpose makes activations non-contiguous, and the heuristic chooses bmm over mm (to avoid a copy). But cuBLAS selects a terrible fp16 bmm kernel (ampere_fp16_s16816gemm) that is 30x slower than the fp32 equivalent. Additionally, max_autotune can't help because '64-bit indexing is not yet implemented for triton templates', so no Triton backend is available.
root_cause.trigger: MultiHeadAttention with batch_first=True, fp16 autocast, small batch size (bs=1-2), on Ampere GPUs

title: Silent incorrectness between static torch.compile vs eager

root_cause.mechanism: When compiling expand+mutation patterns, Inductor infers tensor alignment on first run. On second run with unaligned input, Inductor clones the input to satisfy alignment assumptions, but this clone causes mutations (a[-1] = 3) to be lost — they apply to the clone, not the original. expand() creates a tensor with stride 0, making mutation semantics subtle (writes to all elements in eager).
root_cause.trigger: torch.compile(dynamic=False) on code that does x.expand(n) followed by in-place mutation (a[-1] = 3)
root_cause.confirmed_by: engineer_02 traced: first run gets aligned tensor, second run gets unaligned, clone for alignment loses mutation

diagnostic_path.result: Confirmed inductor-specific
expand() creates stride-0 tensor, so a[-1]=3 writes to ALL elements in eager

diagnostic_path.conclusion: Fake tensor metadata doesn't capture stride-0

diagnostic_path.result: First run aligned → guard set, second run unaligned → clone → mutation lost
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Alignment inference on mutated views is unsafe

title: Compile targets cuda:0 rather than the device the model is on

root_cause.mechanism: Inductor compiles kernels targeting cuda:0 GPU architecture regardless of which device the model is on. On multi-GPU systems with different GPU architectures, the compiled kernel is invalid for the target device.
root_cause.trigger: Multi-GPU system with different GPU models (e.g., GTX 1660 + RTX 3060) where the model is on cuda:1 but Inductor targets cuda:0 architecture
root_cause.confirmed_by: engineer_20 confirmed the bug is real — Inductor sends wrong compilation args; CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES doesn't fix it

diagnostic_path.action: Run torch.compile on cuda:1 with different GPU at cuda:0
diagnostic_path.result: RuntimeError: Triton Error [CUDA]: device kernel image is invalid
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Kernel compiled for wrong GPU arch

diagnostic_path.result: Same error persists
Confirmed kernel loads on correct GPU but compiled with wrong arch args
Affects various GPU combinations (3080+4070ti, 4090+3090)
Issue no longer reproduces

resolution.description: Fixed in later PyTorch versions. Inductor now correctly targets the device the model is on, not always cuda:0.

phase2.extraction_notes: Simple but impactful bug affecting all heterogeneous multi-GPU users. Consumer GPU setups. Note: engineer_20 confirmed GTX 1660 (Turing) should work with Inductor despite not being Volta+.

title: Equivalent index expression results in 50% perf difference in triton kernel

root_cause.mechanism: Inductor generates a less efficient but mathematically equivalent index expression ((5504*x_upper) + (x_lower // 2) vs 2752*x) in Triton kernel for int8 unpacking. The slow form prevents the Triton compiler from inferring that consecutive loads share addresses, leading to full address computation per load, higher register pressure (15→28), and memory loads blocked on address computation.
root_cause.trigger: Int8 tensor unpacking operation where Inductor chooses suboptimal index expression
root_cause.confirmed_by: htyu confirmed via NCU profiling — memory throughput is low for slow kernel because loads are blocked on address computation, not memory bandwidth

diagnostic_path.action: Compare generated kernels for equivalent operations
diagnostic_path.result: Only difference is index expression: (5504*x_upper)+(x_lower//2) vs 2752*x
Slow kernel: 28 registers, fast kernel: 15 registers
diagnostic_path.conclusion: More address arithmetic increases register pressure

diagnostic_path.action: Check memory access patterns in PTX
diagnostic_path.result: Fast kernel reuses addresses for paired loads, slow kernel computes unique addresses
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Compiler can't infer address reuse in slow form

diagnostic_path.result: Slow kernel: loads blocked on address computation (58.67% warp stall on xindex). Fast kernel: loads blocked on memory (21.92%)
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Slow kernel is compute-bound on address calculation, not memory-bound

diagnostic_path.result: Fast path compiler infers x_flat = (xindex // 2) produces same value for every two loads, saving half of address computation
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Triton compiler optimization dependent on index form

resolution.description: Need Inductor to generate simplified index expressions that allow Triton compiler to optimize address computation. The 50% perf difference comes from address computation blocking memory loads, not from memory bandwidth.

phase2.extraction_notes: Exceptional diagnostic depth — NCU profiling, PTX analysis, register pressure analysis. Shows how index expression choice cascades into 50% perf difference via Triton compiler optimization paths.

This was part of a broader gap where compile couldn't handle RNG state operations.

root_cause.confirmed_by: yanboliang reproduced and identified default_generator.get_state() missing source; engineer_21_gh confirmed compile doesn't support activation checkpointing

Generator object doesn't have source attribute for dynamo

title: torch.compile failed on torch.bitwise_xor with constant python number

root_cause.mechanism: bitwise_xor.Scalar (and many other scalar binary ops) are registered as CompositeImplicitAutograd and use wrapped_scalar_tensor internally which creates a tensor without dispatching through FakeTensorMode. This means FakeTensor tracing never intercepts the scalar-to-tensor conversion.
root_cause.trigger: Using torch.bitwise_xor(tensor, python_int) in compiled code — Scalar variant dispatches without going through FakeTensorMode
root_cause.confirmed_by: peterbell10 identified the CompositeImplicitAutograd vs CompositeExplicitAutograd distinction; engineer_20 connected it to broader wrapped_scalar_tensor issue

diagnostic_path.result: FakeTensor error — tensor created without dispatch
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Scalar variant bypasses fake tensor mode

diagnostic_path.result: add.Scalar is CompositeExplicitAutograd, bitwise_xor.Scalar is CompositeImplicitAutograd
bitwise_or, bitwise_xor, many special functions all affected
Community PR submitted

resolution.description: Change scalar binary ops from CompositeImplicitAutograd to CompositeExplicitAutograd so tracing goes through meta implementation instead of the composite that uses wrapped_scalar_tensor without dispatch.

phase2.extraction_notes: Good diagnostic depth on dispatch registration internals. Systematic issue affecting many ops. The distinction between CompositeImplicitAutograd and CompositeExplicitAutograd is an important compile concept.

title: [Dynamo + DDP] DDP partitions FX graph incorrectly with autocast

root_cause.mechanism: DDPOptimizer partitioner has no awareness of autocast enter/exit nodes. When it partitions the graph, autocast regions can be split across subgraphs, causing the entry autocast state of a subgraph to be wrong and leading to accuracy issues.
root_cause.trigger: Using DDP with torch.compile on a model that uses torch.cuda.amp.autocast — the partitioner splits inside an autocast region
root_cause.confirmed_by: wconstab confirmed DDPOptimizer is unaware of autocast nodes; engineer_17 confirmed autocast cache is disabled during tracing making autocast associative and splittable

diagnostic_path.action: Run DDP + compile with autocast model
diagnostic_path.result: Accuracy issues in partitioned subgraphs
Partitioner doesn't know about autocast enter/exit nodes
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Context manager states not preserved across partitions

diagnostic_path.result: Would prevent partitioning inside autocast but blocks DDPOptimizer from splitting large regions

diagnostic_path.action: engineer_17 confirmed autocast cache disabled in tracing
diagnostic_path.result: Autocast is fully associative and can be split
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Splitting is safe, just need to restore state

diagnostic_path.action: Short-term fix: patch autocast/grad state in dynamo
diagnostic_path.result: Unblocked internal model launch
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Short-term fix, long-term plan: kill DDPOptimizer

resolution.description: Short-term fix: patch up autocast/grad context manager state at dynamo level when partitioning. Long-term: replace DDPOptimizer with traceable DDP (collectives in the graph). The partitioner itself was acknowledged as a dead-end — plan to kill it.

phase2.extraction_notes: Important DDP + compile interaction. Blocker for internal model launch. Shows the tension between DDPOptimizer partitioning and context manager state. Long-term plan to kill DDPOptimizer via compiled autograd.

root_cause.confirmed_by: engineer_17 explained the nuance — joint tracing does a calling convention change for views; bdhirsh suggested using aot_export_module instead

Can't use from within torch.compile backend (FakeTensorMode mismatch)

title: torch.compile FakeTensor tracing fails with foreach ops with multiple devices

root_cause.mechanism: Foreach ops (e.g., _foreach_add, _foreach_mul) support multi-device inputs in eager mode, but their meta kernels don't support cross-device operations. FakeTensor tracing fails because meta kernels can't handle tensors on different devices.
root_cause.trigger: Using foreach ops with tensors on different devices (e.g., CUDA + CPU) inside torch.compile — common in multi-device optimizers
root_cause.confirmed_by: zou3519 identified that meta kernels don't support cross-device ops; switching to impl_abstract registration fixes it

diagnostic_path.action: torch.compile with foreach_add on mixed CUDA+CPU tensors
diagnostic_path.result: FakeTensor error — meta kernel can't handle cross-device
SGD works, others fail — some overloads OK but not all
Switch from meta registration to impl_abstract (torch.library.impl_abstract)
Fixed — foreach mixed_dtype tests re-enabled

resolution.description: Switched foreach op registration from meta kernels to torch.library.impl_abstract which supports cross-device operations. Enables multi-device optimizer compilation.

phase2.extraction_notes: Important for optimizer compilation with DDP. The meta vs impl_abstract distinction is a useful concept. Affects all foreach ops, not just foreach_add.

title: DDP+compile+dynamic_shape cause error

root_cause.mechanism: DDPOptimizer (optimize_ddp) is not compatible with dynamic shapes. When the last batch has a different size, dynamo auto-enables symbolic shapes on recompile, but DDPOptimizer can't handle symbolic sizes — errors with 'Cannot call sizes() on tensor with symbolic sizes/strides'.
root_cause.trigger: Using DDP + torch.compile with variable batch sizes (last batch smaller than batch_size) — common in training loops without drop_last=True
root_cause.confirmed_by: wconstab confirmed optimize_ddp not compatible with dynamic shapes; engineer_17 provided workarounds

diagnostic_path.result: Error on last batch (size 7 vs batch_size 16)
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Dynamic batch size triggers the bug

diagnostic_path.result: DDPOptimizer interacts badly with symbolic shapes auto-enabling on recompile
1) dynamic=False on torch.compile, 2) optimize_ddp=False in torch._dynamo.config
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Disable either dynamic shapes or DDP optimizer

diagnostic_path.result: New way of compiling DDP (issue #109774) to replace DDPOptimizer

title: torch.compile() causes accuracy failed: uint8 tensor did not match

root_cause.mechanism: Inductor's batch norm decomposition introduces small numerical differences that, combined with relu's hard edge at zero, cause uint8 tensor comparisons to fail accuracy checks. The accuracy difference is in non-floating-point tensors where small perturbations change relu output from 0 to non-zero or vice versa.
root_cause.trigger: Training MoCo on V100 with torch.compile — accuracy check fails on uint8 tensors
root_cause.confirmed_by: suxiangM identified batch_norm_legit_functional decomposition as source; engineer_02 confirmed non-FP accuracy check is a known false positive

diagnostic_path.result: AccuracyError: Bad accuracy detected
allclose not within tol=0.001
DIVERGED at le: uint8 tensor did not match — relu hard edge

diagnostic_path.action: suxiangM disabled Triton optimization for batch_norm
diagnostic_path.result: Accuracy recovered
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Batch norm decomposition introduces numerical diff

diagnostic_path.result: Test passes — non-FP comparison is false positive
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Not a real accuracy bug, known limitation of uint8 checks

resolution.description: Non-floating-point accuracy checks (uint8 tensors) can produce false positives due to relu hard edges. Set TORCHDYNAMO_REPRO_IGNORE_NON_FP=1 to skip non-FP comparisons. MoCo training passes in dashboard.

phase2.extraction_notes: Good diagnostic workflow using REPRO_AFTER/REPRO_LEVEL. Shows how batch norm decomposition + relu edge = false positive on uint8. Important config: TORCHDYNAMO_REPRO_IGNORE_NON_FP.

root_cause.mechanism: User was using the legacy FX-path quantization (QuantizedTensor-based) which is not supported with Inductor/AOTInductor. The correct path is PT2E quantization which inserts q/dq nodes and works with torch.compile and AOT compilation.

title: [functorch] [aot_autograd] as_strided_scatter error with vmap + aot_function

root_cause.mechanism: Functionalization introduces as_strided_scatter for inplace view operations (e.g., relu_), but as_strided_scatter has no backward support and FakeTensor can't handle the resulting stride patterns. The root cause is that autograd's backward pass uses as_strided as a shortcut for view replay, which doesn't work for tensor subclasses (FakeTensor, BatchedTensor).
root_cause.trigger: Using vmap with aot_function — functionalization of inplace relu produces as_strided_scatter which errors
root_cause.confirmed_by: engineer_17 diagnosed functionalization introducing as_strided_scatter; albanD and engineer_17 agreed on making as_strided support toggleable per tensor subclass

diagnostic_path.action: Create ensemble with vmap, pass to aot_function
diagnostic_path.result: Error in as_strided_scatter
Every relu_() produces as_strided in backward via autograd shortcut
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Autograd view replay shortcut is the issue

diagnostic_path.result: Can prevent as_strided by forcing autograd to generate view functions instead
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Toggle support for as_strided per subclass

diagnostic_path.result: Agreed on making as_strided support toggleable for tensor subclasses
Patches work with the branch

resolution.description: Made as_strided support toggleable per tensor subclass. FakeTensor and BatchedTensor can report they don't support as_strided, causing autograd to use view functions instead of the as_strided shortcut for backward.

phase2.extraction_notes: Deep compiler internals — functionalization + autograd view replay + tensor subclass interaction. The as_strided toggle for subclasses is an important design decision.

title: Key error in index_propagation when looking up dynamic shape variable

root_cause.mechanism: An indirect symbol (indirect0) ends up inside a TypedExpr where it shouldn't be — TypedExpr is supposed to only contain already-known expressions. When index_propagation tries to look up the symbol in var_ranges, it fails because indirect symbols aren't tracked there. Also causes quadratic compilation time from excessive warning logging.
root_cause.trigger: Models with complex indexing patterns (e.g., SwinB encoder) that produce indirect_indexing → index_expr → indirect_indexing chains
root_cause.confirmed_by: lezcano confirmed indirect symbol shouldn't be in TypedExpr; peterbell10 showed valid IR chain that produces it; engineer_17 acknowledged var_to_range should default to unknown

diagnostic_path.result: KeyError in index_propagation for indirect0
indirect0 symbol inside TypedExpr where only known expressions belong
indirect_indexing → index_expr → indirect_indexing produces the problematic state
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Valid but unusual IR, needs graceful handling

diagnostic_path.action: Bandaid fix: default to unknown range
diagnostic_path.result: Fixes crash but produces excessive warnings
diagnostic_path.conclusion: Warning logging causes additional perf issue

diagnostic_path.result: Compilation perf restored for SwinB-like models

resolution.description: Added graceful handling: when a symbol is not in var_ranges, default to unknown range instead of erroring. Also reduced warning verbosity to prevent quadratic compilation time from excessive logging.

phase2.extraction_notes: Compiler internals — index propagation with indirect symbols. Secondary perf issue from warning verbosity. Affects models with complex indexing (SwinB encoder).

root_cause.confirmed_by: jbschlosser confirmed NST not compatible with compile; NJT is the supported path

NST is old layout, NJT works with compile

diagnostic_path.result: Workaround
